When Claude uses grep or bash commands to search your codebase, these bypass the deny rules entirely. Each search can consume massive tokens by scanning thousands of dependency files you never intended to include."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"How do I stop Claude Code from wasting context on node_modules?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Use ripgrep (rg) instead of grep - it respects .gitignore by default and automatically excludes node_modules. Install with 'brew install ripgrep' on macOS or 'sudo apt install ripgrep' on Linux. Add to your CLAUDE.md file instructions to always use rg instead of grep. Alternatively, create a PreToolUse hook that blocks bash commands targeting node_modules."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Why do grep commands bypass Claude Code deny rules?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Claude Code deny rules only apply to the Read() tool. When Claude uses grep or other bash commands via the Bash tool, these execute directly in your shell and bypass Read() deny rules completely. This is why grep can still scan node_modules even with proper deny configuration."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"How do I set up a PreToolUse hook to block node_modules access?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Create .claude/hooks/PreToolUse/block-node-modules.sh with a bash script that checks bash commands for node_modules patterns and returns permissionDecision: deny. Add this hook to .claude/settings.local.json with matcher: Bash. The hook will intercept all bash commands before execution and block any that would access node_modules, vendor, .git, build, or dist directories."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What is ripgrep and why should I use it with Claude Code?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Ripgrep (rg) is a fast search tool that automatically respects .gitignore files by default, meaning it naturally excludes node_modules and other gitignored directories. It's faster than grep and prevents Claude Code from wasting context on dependency directories. Install it and instruct Claude to always use rg instead of grep in your CLAUDE.md file."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"How much context can Claude Code waste on node_modules?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Claude Code can waste a significant amount of the context window reading node_modules directories. JavaScript/TypeScript developers with large node_modules folders can see dramatically higher token consumption than expected. <h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="mobile-advantages">Mobile Advantages<a href="#mobile-advantages" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Mobile Advantages" title="Direct link to Mobile Advantages">​</a></h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Speech-to-speech mode</strong> - Hands-free operation while driving, walking, multitasking</li>
<li><strong>Integrated camera</strong> - Instant photo capture for visual analysis, document scanning, translation</li>
<li><strong>Home screen widgets</strong> (Android) - Quick voice queries without opening app</li>
<li><strong>Siri Shortcuts</strong> (iOS) - Voice activation and automation</li>
<li><strong>Native sharing</strong> - Share content from any app to Claude</li>
<li><strong>Portability</strong> - On-the-spot translations, travel assistance, quick lookups anywhere</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="ad-blank"></div>
<hr>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="web-advantages">Web Advantages<a href="#web-advantages" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Web Advantages" title="Direct link to Web Advantages">​</a></h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Desktop keyboard</strong> - Much faster text input for detailed queries</li>
<li><strong>Keyboard shortcuts</strong> - Sophisticated copy-paste workflows</li>
<li><strong>File system integration</strong> - Drag-and-drop uploads, immediate access to desktop documents</li>
<li><strong>Multi-application workflows</strong> - Easy copy-paste between Claude and text editors or IDEs</li>
<li><strong>Large screen real estate</strong> - Better viewing for complex information and long responses</li>
<li><strong>Multiple tabs</strong> - Juggle research across sources while conversing with Claude</li>
<li><strong>Superior navigation</strong> - Better for reviewing long conversation history</li>
</ul>
